subject: english

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: The stressing of a particular word more than other words in a sentence is referred to as emphatic or contrastive stress. Such a stress normally has its implications in terms of the meaning of the sentence.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
1. JAMES borrowed the novel (i.e James not anybody else borrowed it).
2. James BORROWED the novel (i.e. James didn’t steal or buy the novel, he borrowed it).
3. We MUST honour the invitation (whether we like it or not).
4. I BOUGHT the book (I didn’t steal it).
5. This is THE Mr. Obi (of special fame).

Evaluation: Test on Emphatic stress, page 255, Countdown to English.

learning_objectives: Apply correct pronunciation or stress for Contrasting Stress
Distinguish examples related to Contrasting Stress
